project: MatFlow — Closed-beta readiness sprint (post-RLS): test mocks, admin approval UI, DSAR UI, top 5 audit fixes, member notification persistence
scope: Five discrete release-prep work items from docs/RELEASE-PLAN-2026-05-03.md to take MatFlow from 'RLS shipped, technically working' to 'ready for closed-beta with 5 gyms'. Each story is one acceptance-tested unit. Run on top of `5557173` (RLS migration) which left the test suite with ~111 stale mocks needing the withTenantContext() / withRlsBypass() patterns from lib/prisma-tenant.ts.

stories.title: [DEFERRED] Test mock updates — restore vitest from 143 failures to all-green
stories.deferralReason: User chose to defer per scope conversation 2026-05-03: 143 failures across 35 files is a 1-2 day project; CI doesn't gate Vercel deploys; production unblocked. Tracked for separate dedicated PR.
acceptanceCriteria: npx vitest run exits with code 0 (no failed test files, no failed assertions)
Pass count is at least 295 — must not regress vs the 295/295 baseline that existed before the RLS commit

Test files updated to mock the lib/prisma-tenant.ts surface: vi.mock('@/lib/prisma-tenant', () => ({ withTenantContext: vi.fn((_tenantId, fn) => fn(prismaMock)), withRlsBypass: vi.fn((fn) => fn(prismaMock)) })) where they previously mocked raw prisma directly
No test was deleted or .skip'd to make the suite pass — diff vs HEAD shows only mock signature/wrapper updates and any genuine bugfix
npm run build still passes (no test-only mocks leaking into source)

stories.title: Admin approval UI — application → tenant without curl
acceptanceCriteria: NEW app/admin/applications/page.tsx — super-admin gated (cookie-based MATFLOW_ADMIN_SECRET check or special role); lists pending GymApplication rows with status='pending', shows applicant name/email/gym name/timestamp, has Approve + Reject buttons per row

NEW app/api/admin/applications/[id]/approve/route.ts — POST. Verifies admin secret. Calls existing tenant-creation logic (extract from app/api/admin/create-tenant/route.ts), flips application status to 'approved', sets approvedAt + approvedByAdmin metadata. Sends owner activation email with set-password link.
NEW app/api/admin/applications/[id]/reject/route.ts — POST. Verifies admin secret. Flips status to 'rejected', records reason if provided in body, optionally emails applicant.
Email template addition in lib/email.ts (or templates dir) for owner activation — subject 'Your MatFlow gym is approved', body links to /login/accept-invite?token=... with the password-set magic link
Audit log entry created on both Approve + Reject actions via existing logAudit helper
Vitest unit test: approve creates Tenant + owner User; reject does not; both require admin-secret cookie
npm run build passes; npx tsc --noEmit clean

stories.title: DSAR UI — owner-facing surface for the existing /api/admin/dsar/export endpoint
acceptanceCriteria: NEW app/dashboard/members/[id]/dsar/page.tsx — owner-only (requireRole(['owner'])); shows member name + GDPR-relevant context, has 'Export this member's data (JSON)' button + 'Forget this member (soft-delete + erase)' button with confirm modal
Export button triggers download of JSON via existing /api/admin/dsar/export?memberId=... — frontend wires the download (Blob → anchor click)

Forget button calls /api/admin/dsar/erase?memberId=... (NEW or existing — verify) which soft-deletes the member + scrubs PII fields per UK-GDPR right-to-be-forgotten (name → 'Deleted member', email → null, phone → null, dateOfBirth → null, emergencyContact → null), retains audit trail and aggregate stats. Returns 200 + brief receipt.
Audit log entries via logAudit for both actions: 'dsar.export' + 'dsar.erase' with memberId + ownerId metadata
Vitest unit test: export returns JSON shape, erase scrubs PII while preserving audit references
npm run build passes; npx tsc --noEmit clean

stories.title: Top 5 audit fixes (WP-J + WP-I + WP-C + WP-H + WP-L)
acceptanceCriteria: WP-J: Grep `error: e\.message|error: String\(e\)|error: err\.message` in app/api/**/*.ts returns ZERO matches that send raw error.message back to clients. Routes return generic 'Server error' or specific safe messages; details go to console.error with [route-prefix] context

WP-I: lib/prisma.ts has a boot-time check that warns to console.warn if process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production' AND DATABASE_URL is set AND does not contain 'pgbouncer=true' — surfaces as '[prisma] DATABASE_URL is missing pgbouncer=true&connection_limit=1 — pool exhaustion risk under burst'

WP-C: Resend webhook route (app/api/webhooks/resend/route.ts or wherever the Svix-signed webhook lives) verifies the svix-id, svix-timestamp, svix-signature headers via the svix npm package or hand-rolled HMAC. Unsigned/invalid POSTs return 400 'Invalid signature' without processing the payload

WP-H: app/api/stripe/webhook charge.dispute.lost (or charge.dispute.closed with status='lost') handler now reverses class-pack credit redemption — finds the ClassPackRedemption rows linked to AttendanceRecord rows funded by the disputed charge, increments creditsRemaining back, soft-deletes the redemption row, audit-logs the reversal

WP-L: app/api/admin/create-tenant/route.ts wrapped with checkRateLimit(`admin-create-tenant:${ip}`, 10, 60 * 60 * 1000) — 10 requests per IP per hour. Exceeded returns 429 with Retry-After header. Pre-existing admin-secret check stays as the primary gate.

Vitest unit tests cover: each of the 5 above. WP-J: probe a sample route, confirm error response is generic. WP-I: import lib/prisma in a test with NODE_ENV=production + DATABASE_URL without pgbouncer, expect warn. WP-C: POST to webhook without signature, expect 400. WP-H: simulate dispute_lost event with a known redemption, expect creditsRemaining incremented + redemption row soft-deleted. WP-L: 11th call from same IP returns 429.
npm run build passes; npx tsc --noEmit clean

stories.title: B5 finish — member notification preferences persist
acceptanceCriteria: Schema migration: prisma/migrations/<new>/migration.sql adds three Boolean columns to Member with default true: classReminders, beltPromotions (renamed from 'promotions' to match existing UI label), gymAnnouncements (renamed from 'announcements'). Migration applies cleanly via prisma migrate deploy.
GET /api/member/me returns the three notification fields in its response shape
PATCH /api/member/me accepts a partial { classReminders?: boolean, beltPromotions?: boolean, gymAnnouncements?: boolean } and updates the Member row in tenant-context. Validates types via zod.

app/member/profile/page.tsx hydrates the `notifications` state from the API response on mount (replacing the hardcoded {classReminders: true, promotions: true, announcements: false} default), and the toggle handler PATCHes the API debounced (300ms) — also flips local state optimistically with rollback on failure
Vitest unit test: GET returns defaults; PATCH updates one field at a time; toggle on the page (using @testing-library/react) issues PATCH with correct body
npm run build passes; npx tsc --noEmit clean
